topic: Manipulating arrays in JavaScript using push, pop, and indexing within a p5.js environment.

concepts.name: zero-based indexing
concepts.explanation: The instructor explains that arrays are 'zero indexed', meaning you start counting from 0 rather than 1.
concepts.why_it_matters: If you try to access the first item using index 1, you will actually get the second item or an error.

concepts.name: arr.push()
concepts.explanation: The instructor uses this to 'poke' or add new elements onto the end of an existing array.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to grow a list dynamically during runtime, like adding items to a shopping list.

concepts.name: arr.pop()
concepts.explanation: The instructor describes this as removing elements 'off of the end' of the array.
concepts.why_it_matters: It is useful for removing the most recent item added to a collection.

concepts.name: return values of pop()
concepts.explanation: The instructor demonstrates that pop() doesn't just remove the item, it 'returns' it to whoever called it (like a console.log).
concepts.why_it_matters: This allows you to use the value being removed for other calculations or logging immediately.

concepts.name: NaN (Not a Number)
concepts.explanation: When you try to perform math (like incrementing) on something that doesn't exist or isn't a number, JavaScript returns 'NaN'.
concepts.why_it_matters: It's a signal that you are performing an invalid mathematical operation on the wrong data type.

concepts.name: conditional existence check
concepts.explanation: Using an 'if' statement to check if an array index actually exists before trying to change its value.
concepts.why_it_matters: It prevents the 'NaN' error by ensuring you only perform math on valid, existing elements.

code_examples.demonstrates: Initializing an array with multiple data types (strings and numbers).
Adding a new string to the end of an array.
Removing the last element of an array whenever the mouse is clicked.
code_examples.common_issue: If you click too many times, you might try to pop from an empty array.

code_examples.demonstrates: Removing an element and immediately logging the value that was removed.
Incrementing the value of a specific element in an array.
code_examples.common_issue: If index 1 doesn't exist or isn't a number, you get NaN.

mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Attempting to perform arithmetic on an element that doesn't exist or is not a number.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use an 'if' statement to check if the element exists before incrementing it.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The program doesn't crash but the math is wrong/NaN
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Trying to access an index that has been removed by pop().
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Check the array length or use an 'if (arr[index])' check.

key_moments.description: Visualizing an array as a 'shopping list' to make the abstract concept concrete.
Explaining that pop() returns the value it removes, which is a crucial concept for understanding functions.
The instructor demonstrates the 'NaN' error to show what happens when math is applied to non-numbers.

student_questions: How do I add something to the end of my list?
How do I remove the last item in an array?
Why does my list start at 0 instead of 1?
How can I make the array change when I click the mouse?
How do I see what is inside my array in the console?
Why am I getting errors when I try to change a number in an array?
How do I loop through my list to draw things on the screen?
